title: Chole with Jeera Rice
description: Warm chickpea masala with cumin rice—a pantry-friendly North Indian main for lunch or dinner.
explanation: This is the hot, substantial counterpart to the existing chickpea salad. Use cooked chickpeas for a weeknight version or batch-cook dried chickpeas ahead.
instructions: Cook rice; heat a little oil with cumin and fold it through the cooked rice.
Heat remaining oil and cook onion, ginger-garlic, and tomato until the masala is thick.
Add chole masala, salt, and chickpeas with a splash of water. Simmer 10 minutes.
Mash a few chickpeas against the pan for a thicker gravy if wanted.
Finish with lemon and coriander; serve with jeera rice.
